qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory allocation via default MemoryStorage allows denial-of-service through multipart file upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: the finding identifies default MemoryStorage buffering unbounded file uploads via concat-stream and missing fileSize limits, while the advisory patch fixes async fileFilter cleanup after malformed multipart requests in lib/make-middleware.js.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory is CVE-2026-3304/GHSA-xf7r-hgr6-v32p, a Multer <2.1.0 denial of service from incomplete cleanup on malformed multipart requests; the patch adds cleanup in lib/make-middleware.js when an async fileFilter callback runs after errorOccured. The only final qualifying finding is a separate open-world DoS in default MemoryStorage unbounded buffering.
evidence: Advisory summary: Multer versions <2.1.0 allow denial of service by sending malformed requests, with CWE-459 incomplete cleanup.

Advisory patch 739919097dde3921ec31b930e4b9025036fa74ee changes lib/make-middleware.js to remove the placeholder and resume the file stream when errorOccured is already set inside the fileFilter callback.
The patch test async-file-filter-orphan uses a malformed multipart part with a missing field name and verifies no orphan files remain in the upload directory.
The qualifying finding memory-dos-unbounded-memory-storage instead cites index.js defaulting to memoryStorage(), storage/memory.js piping to concat-stream, and busboy fileSize defaulting to Infinity.
